H1.statement: Higher absorption rate -> lower raw steering effectiveness
H1.conclusion: No significant correlation between absorption rate and raw steering success.

H1b.statement: Higher absorption -> lower delta-corrected steering effectiveness
H1b.conclusion: Significant negative correlation at Layer 8 after random baseline correction. This is the key finding.

H2.statement: Higher absorption rate -> lower sparse probing F1
H2.conclusion: No significant correlation between absorption and probing F1.

H3.statement: Degradation coefficient k is consistent across layers
H3.conclusion: Opposite-sign slopes across layers; degradation is layer-dependent.

H4.conclusion: No significant difference in EC50 between high and low absorption features.

H5.conclusion: Precision is invariant across features; recall varies and drives F1 variance.

key_findings.evidence: H1 shows no correlation (r=+0.008, p=0.97 at L4; r=-0.30, p=0.14 at L8), but H1b shows significant negative correlation at L8 (r=-0.431, p=0.028)
key_findings.implication: Raw steering metrics are confounded by non-specific effects; delta-corrected metrics reveal true absorption-degradation relationship

key_findings.evidence: H1b significant only at Layer 8, not Layer 4. Slopes have opposite signs.
key_findings.implication: Absorption impact varies by layer; deeper layers may be more sensitive

key_findings.evidence: Precision std << Recall std at all k>=5. Most features have precision=1.0.
key_findings.implication: Absorbed features lose coverage (recall) but maintain selectivity (precision)

key_findings.evidence: Feature U (24.2% absorption) achieves 100% steering success at strength=50
key_findings.implication: High steering strength can overcome absorption effects

cross_model_validation.interpretation: Pythia-70m shows comparable absorption rates but no significant absorption-degradation correlation. Likely due to model size (19M vs 124M params) - features are detectable but steering effects are too weak to measure reliably.

limitations: Shallow first-letter features (A-Z) may not generalize to semantic hierarchies
Small sample size (n=26 features) limits statistical power
Only two downstream tasks tested (steering and probing)
Cross-model validation on Pythia-70m inconclusive due to model size limitations
